revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period burial mounds. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/山の辺の道-大神神社ほか/珠城山古墳群/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.narahaku.go.jp/collection/728-5.html Nara National Museume]{{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period burial mounds. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/山の辺の道-大神神社ほか/珠城山古墳群/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.narahaku.go.jp/collection/728-5.html Nara National Museume]{{in lang|ja}}

'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period burial mounds. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period burial mounds. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period burial mounds. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. '''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period burial mounds. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period burial mounds. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/山の辺の道-大神神社ほか/珠城山古墳群/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.narahaku.go.jp/collection/728-5.html Nara National Museume]{{in lang|ja}}

[[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/山の辺の道-大神神社ほか/珠城山古墳群/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.narahaku.go.jp/collection/728-5.html Nara National Museume]{{in lang|ja}}

[[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[haji ware]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[Makimuku Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[:en:Makimuku Station|Makimuku Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/山の辺の道-大神神社ほか/珠城山古墳群/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.narahaku.go.jp/collection/728-5.html Nara National Museume]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]]

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[sue ware]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[:en:Makimuku Station|Makimuku Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/山の辺の道-大神神社ほか/珠城山古墳群/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.narahaku.go.jp/collection/728-5.html Nara National Museume]{{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]]

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | native_name =珠城山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|47|N|135|50|54|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tamakiyama Kofun Cluster'''|珠城山古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Anashi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191432|title=珠城山古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tamakiyama Kofun cluster is located on the edge of a hill where a branch of Mount Anashi, a separate peak of Mount Makimuku, extends west, to the north of Mount Miwa on the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out five times since 1955, and the tombs were noted for the luxurious nature of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 1''' is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the east and had a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] that opens to the south. The tumulus is 53 meters long, 20 meters in diameter at the posterior circular portion, and 20 meters wide at the anterior rectangular portion. The burial chamber is 3. 4 meters long, 1.65 meters wide at the back wall, and about 2 meters high. In the center of the burial chamber, a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] composite stone coffin was placed along the axis, and inside the coffin were some human bones, knives, and small glass beads. Other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were outside the coffin, including [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery placed along the back wall, horse equipment and a ring-headed iron sword, tools, gilt bronze ''[[magatama]]'', silver hollow beads, amber beaded caddies, on the east side of the coffin, and armor and more saddle fittings on the west side of the coffin. The construction period iof this tumulus s thought to be the middle to late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2''' is a west-facing ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus with a total length of 75 meters, anterior rectangular portion 40 meters wide, and a posterior circular portion 40 meters in diameter, making it the largest of the group. The interior of the posterior circular portion has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Tamakiyama Kofun No. 3''', was also a ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' tumulus that was located to the west of Tamakiyama Kofun No. 2. It was mostly leveled in 1958, leaving only a part of the anterior rectangular portion. There are one side-entry stone burial chamber in each of the front and rear mounds, and gilt bronze double phoenix design with openwork apricot leaves earrings, sword fragments, and pottery have been excavated from the front burial chamber. The burial chamber in the rear mound was 9.7 meters long and contained a combination stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The group of tombs was probably built in the order of Tomb No. 2, No. 3, and No. 1, and is considered to be part of a series of early and middle Kofun period Kofuns. The site is about 20 minute walk from [[:en:Makimuku Station|Makimuku Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/山の辺の道-大神神社ほか/珠城山古墳群/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.narahaku.go.jp/collection/728-5.html Nara National Museume]{{in lang|ja}}
